title: agropyron subsecundum
intro: Agropyron subsecundum, also known as crested wheatgrass, is a perennial grass native to Eurasia and North America, valued for its drought tolerance and nutritional properties. It is rich in antioxidants, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, which provide medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral activities. The health benefits of crested wheatgrass include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce oxidative stress, and exhibit potential anticancer properties. The plant can be prepared as an herbal tea, tincture, or dietary supplement, and is also used as a natural food source for livestock.
